Evan Reese, Esq., VARELA, LEE, METZ & GUARINO, LLP 415-623-7000 ereese@vlmglaw.com, for Plaintiff Webcor Construction L.P. (Personal appearance)

Off Calendar Feb-18-2022 Continued To Apr-06-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CDC SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

The court sua sponte stays this action based on CCP 128(a)(8) [inherent powers] and the purpose of CCP 1281.4 [stay after compelling arbitration]. The parties are presently arbitrating the instant indemnity dispute and there is no purpose for two forums to address the issue. (Troughton Decl., Exs. J-L [demand for arbitration and answer].) "The purpose of the statutory stay [required pursuant to section 1281.4] is to protect the jurisdiction of the arbitrator by preserving the status quo until arbitration is resolved. [Citations.] [] In the absence of a stay, the continuation of the proceedings in the trial court disrupts the arbitration proceedings and can render them ineffective. [Citation.]" (Federal Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 1370, 1374-1375.) Allowing this action to continue while the arbitration proceeds is inefficient and undermines the authority of the arbitrator.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2022 Continued To Feb-18-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
